best_fields.question: Who is Arthas Menethil?
best_fields.reference: Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron and a Knight of the Silver Hand. He became a death knight after claiming the cursed runeblade Frostmourne in Northrend, sacrificing his soul in the process. He eventually became the Lich King after merging with Ner'zhul, and ruled from the Frozen Throne in Icecrown Citadel until he was defeated by a group of adventurers.

He was trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and was inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand. Arthas also had a romantic relationship with the kind sorceress Jaina Proudmoore. Committed to the protection of his people, Arthas was determined to stop the plague spreading throughout Lordaeron. Despite his promising beginnings, Arthas became one of the most powerful and evil beings Azeroth would ever know. Although he killed the necromancer Kel'Thuzad, Arthas was unable to stop the spread of the plague and resorted to increasingly desperate methods, such as purging the city of Stratholme. Taking up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne, Arthas was able to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, but he lost his soul in the process. Becoming a death knight of the Scourge, he led the undead in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran. Arthas later made his way to the Frozen Throne of Icecrown and merged with the Lich King.March Badness Ruling as the Lich King for years afterward, Arthas had the last remnants of his humanity destroyed and was ultimately defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. Cradled by the spirit of his father, Arthas Menethil died, leaving the mantle of the Lich King to be taken by a noble soul who would contain the power of the Scourge.

# Arthas Menethil

**Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron who became one of Azeroth's most powerful and evil beings.** Born as the son of King Terenas Menethil II, he was trained as a paladin by Uther the Lightbringer and showed great promise as a protector of his people. He even had a romantic relationship with the sorceress Jaina Proudmoore.

However, his descent into darkness came when he desperately tried to stop a devastating plague spreading through Lordaeron. Unable to contain it through conventional means, Arthas resorted to increasingly brutal methods, including the purging of the city of Stratholme. He took up the cursed runeblade **Frostmourne** and defeated the demon Mal'Ganis, but at a terrible cost—he lost his soul in the process. This transformed him into a death knight of the Scourge.

**Arthas then led the undead armies in destroying Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran, eventually making his way to the Frozen Throne where he merged with the Lich King.** He ruled as the Lich King for years, with his remaining humanity stripped away, until he was finally defeated in combat by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions of the Light. He died cradled by the spirit of his father, passing the mantle of the Lich King to another.

# Frostmourne: The Blade That Shaped History

**Frostmourne was the legendary runeblade of the Lich King**, a fearsome two-handed longsword inscribed with dark runes and capable of shattering souls. The sword possessed terrifying power—it could imprison the souls of those it struck and transform the living into mindless undead. Created by the Runecarver at the behest of the Jailer, Frostmourne was thrust from the Frozen Throne and claimed by Arthas Menethil during the Third War when he discovered it in an ice-encased cavern in Northrend.

**Arthas wielded Frostmourne throughout his transformation into a death knight and later as the Lich King himself.** During his reign, the blade spilled the blood of kings, destroyed entire nations, and left scars on the world that persist to this day. It was eventually destroyed by Tirion Fordring during the confrontation at the Frozen Throne. However, Frostmourne's legacy didn't end there—during the Burning Legion's third invasion, a death knight hero reforged the shattered remains of the blade into the **Blades of the Fallen Prince**, ensuring that this world-altering weapon's power lived on in new form.

best_fields.question: What is the Wrathgate?
best_fields.reference: The Wrathgate, also known as Angrathar the Wrathgate, is a massive iron gate that serves as the entrance to Icecrown from the Dragonblight in Northrend. It was the site of a major battle where Horde and Alliance forces fought against the Scourge. The battle was interrupted when a faction of undead called the Forsaken Royal Apothecary Society unleashed a plague that killed both Scourge and Alliance and Horde forces, including High Overlord Saurfang the Younger.

Angrathar the Wrathgate is a barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel and is located in the northwest of Dragonblight. It is a towering wall of knife edges made from saronite. During the war against the Lich King, the battle at the Wrath Gate was to be the first major blow against the Lich King, and the first in which the Alliance and Horde fought side by side instead of against each other. History Wrath of the Lich King thumb|Warlord Dranosh Saurfang and Highlord Bolvar Fordragon fighting the undead at the Wrathgate. During the war against the Lich King, the Wrath Gate was under siege by both the Alliance and the Horde forces, which set up the bases of Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard. Respectively led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang, their forces intended to defeat the Scourge forces at the gate and to storm into Icecrown Citadel. Their campaign culminated in the disastrous Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate, where they faced the Lich King himself, before being betrayed by Grand Apothecary Putress and his allies, who betrayed the Horde and all present by unleashing the New Plague on all the gathered forces. As the plague spread through the battlefield, many Horde and Alliance attempted to flee. The Lich King was also in serious danger of succumbing to the plague, and only barely managed to escape back through the Wrathgate.

# The Wrathgate

**Angrathar the Wrathgate is a towering barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel**, located in northwest Dragonblight. It's constructed from massive knife-edged walls of saronite and served as the focal point of the war against the Lich King during the Wrath of the Lich King expansion.

The gate became infamous as the site of the **Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate**, a catastrophic battle where the Alliance and Horde fought together for the first time against a common enemy. Led by Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang respectively, their combined forces launched an assault on the Scourge defenses, intending to breach Icecrown Citadel itself. However, the battle ended in disaster when **Grand Apothecary Putress betrayed both factions by unleashing the New Plague** across the battlefield. The plague spread indiscriminately, forcing both armies to retreat. Even the Lich King himself barely escaped back through the Wrathgate before being overwhelmed by the plague.

The Wrathgate remains one of the most pivotal and tragic moments in World of Warcraft's history, marking both the peak of Alliance-Horde cooperation and its brutal collapse through treachery.

best_fields.response:
**Twinking** in World of Warcraft refers to the practice of gearing up a low-level character with high-quality equipment from your higher-level characters or the auction house. Twinks are typically created to dominate in low-level PvP battlegrounds by wearing powerful gear that's well above what normal players at that level would have access to.

The strategy involves equipping a character with items that are either heirloom gear (which scales with your level), crafted gear from high-level professions, or gear acquired from dungeons and raids that you've already outleveled. This gives twinks a massive advantage in damage, survivability, and stats compared to other players leveling naturally through the same content.

Twinking became especially popular in PvP-focused brackets like level 19 and 29 battlegrounds, where the gear disparity could be most pronounced. Blizzard has implemented various changes over the years to address twinking, including adding experience bars to twinks and adjusting how heirloom scaling works, but the practice remains a common way for experienced players to enjoy low-level PvP with an edge.

# The Core Difference Between Horde and Alliance

**Horde and Alliance are the two opposing factions** that players choose between when creating a character in World of Warcraft. This is the fundamental divide that structures the entire game's PvP, story, and social experience.

The **Alliance** is composed of the "good" races—Humans, Dwarves, Night Elves, Draenei, and Worgen—and represents the civilized, "civilized" forces of Azeroth. The **Horde** consists of the "savage" or outcasted races—Orcs, Tauren, Trolls, Undead, and Blood Elves—and represents those who've banded together for survival and power despite their differences.

Beyond lore, the factions are **mechanically separate**. You cannot group with or trade with the opposite faction (with limited exceptions), cannot use the same cities, and experience different questlines and stories. This creates two parallel narratives throughout the game, with quests and zones often having Horde and Alliance versions that tell the same events from opposing perspectives. Your choice of faction fundamentally shapes which content you see, which players you interact with, and which side of Warcraft's ongoing conflicts you'll fight for.

best_fields.question: Who is Sylvanas Windrunner?
best_fields.reference: Sylvanas Windrunner is a former Ranger-General of Silvermoon who was killed by Arthas Menethil and raised as a banshee. She eventually regained her body and became the leader of the Forsaken undead faction. She later became Warchief of the Horde after Garrosh Hellscream and Vol'jin. She is known for burning Teldrassil and for her role in the Shadowlands storyline where she shattered the Helm of Domination.
snippets: Sylvanas Windrunner: Lady Sylvanas Windrunner, formerly styled "the Dark Lady" and "the Banshee Queen", is the former Warchief of the Horde and former supreme ruler of the Forsaken, one of the most powerful factions of undead on Azeroth. In life, Sylvanas was the ranger-general of Silvermoon, whose leadership acumen and martial prowess were without equal. During the Third War, she bravely defended Quel'Thalas from a Scourge invasion led by the death knight Arthas Menethil. Ultimately, however, Sylvanas fell in battle. Rather than honor the ranger-general with a quick death, Arthas ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee: a cunning and vengeful agent of the Lich King empowered by hate. When the Lich King's control over his minions weakened, Sylvanas broke away from her tyrannical master's control and reclaimed her body. Vowing to avenge her death, Sylvanas gathered other renegade undead and set out to wage war against the Scourge. Thus it was that the Forsaken came to be, with Sylvanas as their queen. Under Sylvanas' command, the Forsaken joined the Horde and later helped bring about the Lich King's fall in the frozen wastes of Northrend. She seeks a higher purpose for a people who have already died once.The Characters of Warcraft/Sylvanas Windrunner Yet many challenges still lay ahead for Sylvanas. After an uprising within her ranks that killed other members of the Horde, the banshee queen is now mistrusted by many of her allies.

Sylvanas Windrunner (tactics): Sylvanas Windrunner is the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination. The encounter is an epic battle that takes its start at the Pinnacle of Domination before continuing across the Edge of the Abyss into the Crucible in Oribos, all while Sylvanas tries to waylay the heroes of Azeroth and prevent them from interfering with the Jailer's plan. Adventure Guide With Oribos in peril, a desperate battle at the pinnacle of the Sanctum begins. Standing between the Jailer and the mortals who would stop him is Sylvanas Windrunner. With no masks left to wear, the Banshee Queen unleashes her full power to protect the Banished One while his ultimate plan unfolds. Overview Azeroth's champions battle Sylvanas at the pinnacle of the Sanctum of Domination, across the vastness between the Maw and the Eternal City, and within the Arbiter's chamber. Players first encounter Sylvanas atop Torghast. Upon reaching 85% health, she rips apart chunks of the tower itself. As the Jailer envelops Oribos in the Maw, players pursue him with the aid of Thrall, Jaina Proudmoore, and Bolvar Fordragon, forging new paths and defeating Mawforged commanders while Sylvanas harrows from all sides in Banshee Form. Within the Crucible, the Banished One executes his grand plan. Our allies confront the dominated Anduin Wrynn while Sylvanas engages players directly. # Sylvanas Windrunner

**Sylvanas Windrunner is one of World of Warcraft's most pivotal and complex characters.** In life, she served as the Ranger-General of Silvermoon, the capital of the blood elves, commanding their military with unmatched skill and leadership. During the Third War, she valiantly defended Quel'Thalas against a Scourge invasion led by the death knight Arthas Menethil. When she fell in battle, Arthas didn't grant her a swift end—instead, he ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee, binding her to serve as one of the Lich King's agents.

**Sylvanas eventually broke free from the Lich King's control and reclaimed her body,** driven by a vow to avenge her own death. She gathered other renegade undead and founded the Forsaken, a powerful faction that became one of the Horde's most significant members. Under her rule as the "Dark Lady" and "Banshee Queen," the Forsaken fought against the Scourge and helped bring about the Lich King's downfall in Northrend.

However, her story took a dark turn. An uprising within her own ranks shattered the trust her allies had placed in her, and she became increasingly mistrusted by the Horde leadership. This ultimately led to her becoming a major antagonist, culminating in an epic confrontation where she served as the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination, defending the Jailer's plans while having discarded all pretense of restraint.

Lich King (tactics): The Lich King is the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and the final major encounter of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King. This boss has been available since February 2, 2010 on US servers and February 3 on EU servers. The Lich King is a long and challenging battle for survival. All classes are tested to the limits of their abilities, in particular all raid members have to react quickly to various abilities, often several at the same time. The encounter is rather unforgiving; most players must react properly in various situations, and all players get ample opportunity to commit mistakes which then wipe the raid. This encounter is a fitting end to Wrath of the Lich King and on par with Yogg-Saron in Ulduar. Abilities The Lich King melees for roughly 40,000 on an item level 264 geared tank on 25-player difficulty. The Lich King enrages after 15 minutes of combat, gaining 900% damage and 150% attack speed. However, he is notably still affected by taunt abilities. Phase 1 Phase 1.5 and 2.5 Phase 2 The Lich King retains Infest. Phase 3 The Lich King retains Soul Reaper and Defile. Frostmourne Spirit Warden Terenas Menethil Phase 4 Terenas Menethil Preparation The fight requires the standard raid composition of 2/3 Tanks and 3/5 healers (10/25 player), while learning the encounter more healers can help. No resistance or other special gear is required.

# The Lich King

The **Lich King** is the master and lord of the Scourge—an undead army that he controlled telepathically through the Helm of Domination from his throne atop Icecrown Citadel. He was originally created by Kil'jaeden of the Burning Legion, who crafted the being from the spirit of the orc shaman **Ner'zhul** as a weapon to weaken Azeroth before the Legion's invasion.

The Lich King eventually betrayed his creator and **merged with the human prince Arthas Menethil**, becoming the expansion's primary antagonist in *Wrath of the Lich King*. After Arthas was defeated by Highlord Tirion Fordring and his champions, **Bolvar Fordragon** took the mantle to contain the Scourge. The position ultimately ended when **Sylvanas Windrunner** defeated Bolvar and destroyed the Helm of Domination itself.

Death Knights and the Scourge remained intrinsically bound to the Lich King's consciousness—his will literally drove them forward as instruments of his power. The Lich King served as the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and represented the ultimate challenge of the *Wrath* expansion.

# Illidan Stormrage: The Betrayer

**Illidan Stormrage is known as one of World of Warcraft's most iconic and complex villains.** He's a demon hunter and former Night Elf who became a major antagonist, particularly in the Burning Crusade expansion where he ruled the Black Temple in Outland. His defining characteristic is his relentless pursuit of power against the Burning Legion, though his methods—including the use of fel magic and demonic corruption—earned him the title "The Betrayer."

**He's renowned for creating the Illidari, an army of demon hunters imbued with demonic powers similar to his own, who wielded warglaives like his legendary Warglaives of Azzinoth.** These demon hunters served as his elite forces in his campaign against the Legion. Despite his antagonistic role, Illidan became a more sympathetic character over time, revealed to be fighting the Legion in his own ruthless way, though his methods often put him at odds with other forces in Azeroth—particularly Akama and Maiev Shadowsong, who conspired against him.

His legacy extends across multiple expansions, with players eventually hunting down his soul and confronting him as one of World of Warcraft's most memorable raid bosses in the Black Temple.

# Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker

**Thunderfury is the legendary sword that once belonged to Thunderaan, Prince of Air.** This iconic weapon has a rich history woven into World of Warcraft's lore. After Ragnaros the Firelord defeated Thunderaan during the Elemental Sundering, he trapped what remained of the Prince's essence in an elemental talisman and shattered it. Ages later, a follower of Thunderaan named Highlord Demitrian recombined the talisman pieces and crafted Thunderfury as a vessel to hold his master's essence.

**The blade is significant because it's the only legendary weapon available to all classes in WoW.** Players who equip it earn the prestigious Feat of Strength achievement. Throughout the game's storyline, Thunderfury has been wielded by various characters, including a dwarf warrior named Wray who carried it to the Broken Isles before his death. The weapon later played a crucial role when the Earthen Ring decided to resurrect Thunderaan to replace the fallen Al'Akir — a shaman adventurer retrieved it and brought it to the Vortex Pinnacle for this purpose.

Beyond the legendary weapon itself, there's also a humorous item called the **Inflatable Thunderfury** that was available during WoW's anniversaries, rewarding the achievement "Did Someone Say...?" when used.

# Arthas Menethil: The Fall of a Prince

**Arthas Menethil was the Crown Prince of Lordaeron who became one of Azeroth's greatest villains.** Born as the son of King Terenas Menethil II, he was trained as a paladin by the legendary Uther the Lightbringer and inducted into the Order of the Silver Hand. He was romantically involved with Jaina Proudmoore and initially showed genuine commitment to protecting his people from the plague spreading across Lordaeron.

However, his desperate attempts to stop the undead plague led him down a dark path. After killing the necromancer Kel'Thuzad failed to stem the tide, Arthas resorted to increasingly brutal methods—most infamously purging the city of Stratholme of its own citizens. When he took up the cursed runeblade Frostmourne to defeat the demon Mal'Ganis, he paid the ultimate price: his soul. He became a death knight of the Scourge and systematically destroyed Lordaeron, Quel'Thalas, and Dalaran.

Few weapons in World of Warcraft history have shaped the fate of Azeroth as profoundly as Frostmourne.

# The Wrathgate

The **Wrathgate**, officially called **Angrathar the Wrathgate**, is a massive barred entrance to Icecrown Citadel located in northwestern Dragonblight. It's a towering wall of knife-like edges constructed from saronite that served as the final barrier between the mortal forces and the Lich King's seat of power.

During the **Wrath of the Lich King expansion**, the Wrathgate became the site of a historic moment when the Alliance and Horde united against a common enemy for the first time. Both factions established bases—Fordragon Hold and Kor'kron Vanguard—under the command of Highlord Bolvar Fordragon and Warlord Dranosh Saurfang respectively. Their combined forces launched a siege against the Scourge, intending to break through the gate and storm Icecrown Citadel itself.

However, this cooperation ended in tragedy. The **Battle of Angrathar the Wrathgate** became one of WoW's most pivotal disasters when **Grand Apothecary Putress** betrayed both factions by unleashing the **New Plague** across the entire battlefield. The weapon was so devastating that even the Lich King himself barely escaped back through the gate. This treacherous moment shattered the Alliance-Horde alliance and reshaped the course of the war against the Lich King.

rrf_elser.response:
# Horde vs. Alliance: The Core Divide

**Horde and Alliance are the two opposing factions in World of Warcraft**, and the choice between them fundamentally shapes your entire gameplay experience. Your character's race determines which faction you join—most races are locked to one side, though Pandaren can choose either faction.

**The gameplay differences are substantial.** Each faction has access to different settlements, NPCs, quests, and reputation factions. You'll level through distinct questlines, raid different dungeons, and encounter unique storylines. The faction system also prevents cross-faction interaction—Horde and Alliance players can't communicate or form groups together (with limited exceptions in modern expansions). Most PvP gameplay pits the factions directly against each other, making them natural enemies on the battlefield.

**Narratively, the Horde and Alliance represent opposing philosophies and alliances of different races.** The Alliance traditionally encompasses the "civilized" races like Humans and Dwarves, while the Horde brought together outcasts like Orcs and Undead. Their relationship has evolved throughout WoW's history from outright warfare to temporary truces to the current "uneasy armistice" following the Fourth War. The factional conflict—through story, mechanics, and PvP—is the core element that defines the entire World of Warcraft experience, creating a persistent sense of being part of a larger side in an ongoing struggle.

# Sylvanas Windrunner

**Sylvanas Windrunner is one of World of Warcraft's most complex and powerful characters.** In life, she was the ranger-general of Silvermoon, an elf of extraordinary martial skill and leadership. During the Third War, she heroically defended Quel'Thalas against Arthas Menethil's Scourge invasion, but ultimately fell in battle. Rather than grant her a merciful death, Arthas ripped out her soul and transformed it into a banshee—a vengeful spirit enslaved to the Lich King.

When the Lich King's grip weakened, Sylvanas broke free from his control and reclaimed her body, vowing vengeance against the Scourge. She gathered other renegade undead and founded **the Forsaken**, becoming their queen and joining the Horde. Under her leadership, the Forsaken played a crucial role in defeating the Lich King in Northrend. However, her rule has been marked by controversy—an uprising within her ranks that killed other Horde members left her deeply mistrusted by her allies.

Sylvanas eventually became **Warchief of the Horde**, though this period was defined by increasingly questionable decisions. She served as the final boss of the Sanctum of Domination raid, where she fought alongside the Jailer to prevent the champions of Azeroth from stopping his plans. Her story is one of tragedy, survival, and the constant struggle to find purpose for a people who have already died once.

Matthias tried to reason with Arthas, but Arthas silenced him by running him through with Frostmourne. Ner'zhul was delighted by this, declaring that he and Arthas were now free to merge into a single glorious being. Arthas rejected this offer, stating that once he had the power of the Lich King, no one would tell him what to do again. He impaled the stunned Ner'zhul with Frostmourne, becoming the dominant personality of the Lich King and ending the dream.Arthas: Rise of the Lich King, Epilogue In his quiet meditation, the part of the Lich King that was Arthas began to reflect on the events of his life, on the path that had taken him so far from the young boy who who had been his father's pride and joy. In the midst of his reflections, Arthas sensed a bitter and ancient presence locked within the nearby ice, and with this realization came the name Sindragosa.The Cinematic Art of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King Wrath of the Lich King Awakening thumb|upright=1.2|The awakened Lich King and his undead armies in Icecrown.

Lich King (tactics): The Lich King is the final boss of Icecrown Citadel and the final major encounter of World of Warcraft: Wrath of the Lich King. This boss has been available since February 2, 2010 on US servers and February 3 on EU servers. The Lich King is a long and challenging battle for survival. All classes are tested to the limits of their abilities, in particular all raid members have to react quickly to various abilities, often several at the same time. The encounter is rather unforgiving; most players must react properly in various situations, and all players get ample opportunity to commit mistakes which then wipe the raid. This encounter is a fitting end to Wrath of the Lich King and on par with Yogg-Saron in Ulduar. Abilities The Lich King melees for roughly 40,000 on an item level 264 geared tank on 25-player difficulty. The Lich King enrages after 15 minutes of combat, gaining 900% damage and 150% attack speed. However, he is notably still affected by taunt abilities. Phase 1 Phase 1.5 and 2.5 Phase 2 The Lich King retains Infest. Phase 3 The Lich King retains Soul Reaper and Defile. Frostmourne Spirit Warden Terenas Menethil Phase 4 Terenas Menethil Preparation The fight requires the standard raid composition of 2/3 Tanks and 3/5 healers (10/25 player), while learning the encounter more healers can help. No resistance or other special gear is required.

Icon of the Lich King: thumb|250px|Icon of the Lich King. The Icon of the Lich King is the sigil of the Scourge. Its azure coloration and icy ornamentation symbolize the frigid homeland of the undead horde in Northrend. At the icon's center is the now-shattered runeblade Frostmourne, the former blade of the Lich King Arthas Menethil. The frost-covered hammers flanking the blade represent the Scourge's endless capacity for warfare, as well as their construction of the saronite citadel Icecrown and the dread Necropoli that shepherded the endless armies of the dead and served as hubs of unspeakable torment upon the living. Although the holder of the Helm of Domination has changed since the creation of the sigil, and the goals of the Scourge along with them, this sigil seems to have remained representative of the organization. The Cult of the Damned, an affiliate group consisting primarily of the living, retains both a unique icon and the twisted ambitions of the original Scourge. Gallery See also Icon Category:Icons

rrf_elser.response:
# The Lich King

**The Lich King is the supreme ruler of the Scourge**, an undead army that plagued Azeroth. The entity is actually a composite consciousness born from multiple personalities fighting for dominance: **Arthas Menethil** (a corrupted death knight prince), **Ner'zhul** (an orc shaman), and **Matthias Lehner** (the remnants of Arthas's humanity).

During a period of inner conflict, **Arthas emerged as the dominant personality**, destroying both Matthias and Ner'zhul to become the sole consciousness of the Lich King. He wielded the legendary runeblade **Frostmourne** and ruled from **Icecrown Citadel** in Northrend, commanding endless armies of undead through the power of the **Helm of Domination**.

The Lich King served as the **final boss of the Wrath of the Lich King expansion**, representing the ultimate threat players faced during that era. His sigil—the **Icon of the Lich King**—features Frostmourne at its center, flanked by frost-covered hammers symbolizing the Scourge's endless capacity for warfare and their construction of Icecrown and the necropoli that served as bases for their undead legions.

rrf_elser.question: What is Illidan Stormrage known for?
rrf_elser.reference: Illidan Stormrage is a night elf demon hunter and the twin brother of Malfurion Stormrage. He is known for drinking from the Skull of Gul'dan which transformed him into a demon-like being. He was imprisoned for ten thousand years for creating a new Well of Eternity. After being freed he took control of Outland and the Black Temple. He is often associated with the quote 'You are not prepared.' He was later revealed to be a crucial weapon against the Burning Legion.
snippets: Illidan Stormrage: While Malfurion had spent a lifetime fighting for the dream of what Azeroth could be, he now must fight for what it is. With his final words, Illidan asked Malfurion to take care of Tyrande and listen to her counsel, for she was always the best of them. The road ahead would be long, but whatever came, he knew Malfurion would bring honor to the name Stormrage. To Tyrande, he spoke of how all the paths he had taken had led to a single purpose, saving the world; he could bide no half measures or compromises. He told her how whenever he faced doubt, he held on to one anchor: Her. He told Tyrande that she embodied the best of Azeroth with her faith and devotion, and asked her to take care of Malfurion. While he sometimes wished her heart had made a different choice, in the end he knew she made the right one. Atop Hyjal, on the shores of the Well of Eternity that he created ages ago, Illidan told the adventurer that they had proven their commitment to Azeroth, that their dedication and sacrifice rivaled his own.

The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage: The Hunt for Illidan Stormrage is the continuation of the Illidan Stormrage campaign in Patch 7.1.0. Objectives Seek out Allari the Souleater at Illidari Stand in Azsuna. Description Allari the Souleater, an oracle of the Illidari, has been on the hunt for Illidan's soul for quite some time. She now believes that she has discovered its location and requires your assistance in its recovery. Find Allari at Illidari Stand in Azsuna and aid her in any way possible. If Sargeras truly plans to use Illidan's body as a vessel for his own return then returning Illidan's soul to his body is integral in preventing the dark titan from returning to our world! Rewards You will receive: Completion The naaru heard my call? Interesting... Very well, let's get to work. We've found Illidan's soul - finally - and it's not where we expected it to be held. Progression Patch changes External links

Illidan Stormrage (tactics): Illidan Stormrage is the final boss of the Black Temple. The fight consists of five phases, and good positioning is the key to success. Biography Adventure Guide Overview Illidan wields his Warglaives at the start of the encounter, infecting foes with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Upon reaching 65% health, Illidan takes flight and throws the Warglaives of Azzinoth to the ground, each of which unleashes a Flame. The two Flames of Azzinoth must be defeated to bring Illidan to the ground. For the remainder of the encounter, Illidan periodically unleashes the power of the demonic force within him, using those dark powers to assault his foes, before returning to his natural form. At 30% health, Maiev arrives to assist in handling the Betrayer's final efforts to overpower his foes. file:Dps_icon.png Damage Dealers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Healer_icon.png Healers Move away from allies when afflicted with Parasitic Shadowfiend. Quickly defeat the Flames of Azzinoth in Stage Two of the encounter. file:Tank_icon.png Tanks Use Active Mitigation defensive abilities to survive Shear. Move Illidan Stormrage to avoid damage taken from Flame Crash. In Stage Two, be quick to grab the attention of the Flames of Azzinoth.

Lord Illidan Stormrage (quest): Lord Illidan Stormrage is the second of the three storyline quests, available starting at reputation with Netherwing. Objectives Meet Illidan Stormrage. Seek out Barthamus in the Lower City of Shattrath should you survive. You will need: Meeting with Illidan Stormrage Description Come, highlord . Illidan himself awaits us! Completion I was the first, you know. The abandoned child of a monster... We owe you everything, . You have single-handedly shattered the Dragonmaw empire and gathered enough information to keep my brethren occupied for ten lifetimes. It is not much but there is something that we can offer... My kin have each offered to join you on your mission in Outland. Simply ask and they will bond with you. Quest Dialogue During this quest, Overlord Mor'ghor summons an image of Illidan at the Dragonmaw Base Camp to meet the "Dragonmaw orc" (the player in disguise) who will be their champion: An image of Illidan appears. Illidan immobilizes you. Illidan's image vanishes. Yarzill the Merc transforms into a nether dragon! Notes thumb|Riding off into the sunset on Yarzill's back At the end of the dialogue sequence, Yarzill transforms into a nether dragon and carries the player to Shattrath City. The trip is slow, as Yarzill flies at about walking speed. It is advised to have the necessary time before triggering this quest, although as of patch 2.3.0 the speed has been raised.

Awakenings: Objectives Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage in Val'sharah. Witness the Awakening of Illidan Stormrage Description Light's Heart is to serve as the vessel for Illidan Stormrage's rebirth: my last act of service to the Light. But Illidan is dead and his soul is lost in the Nether. Before we may attempt to recover his soul, we must prepare the vessel for his return. Across two worlds exist echoes of Illidan's past. You must venture out and find the echoes. Start where all life begins, : birth. Find Illidan's birthplace in Val'sharah. Awaken the memory. Rewards You will receive: Progress When our work is complete, I will be lost to the Great Dark. Completion An auspicious start to a life of tragedy. Perhaps one of the few joyous memories Illidan would have in his life. While the years that followed tested the prophesied child, they would never break him. You must remember that, despite what you may see as our journey continues. There is much more to be done, but I am not yet ready. I will call for you when it is time. Gains 20,560 XP Notes Go to Lorlathil in Val'sharah. This will prompt a message from Xe'ra. Press the extra action button to see the cinematic. thumb|Elves in front of the newborn Stormrage twins. A crowd of night elves has assembled in Lorlathil around two cribs, presided by a lead Priestess of the Moon wearing the Raiment of Divine Clarity set, and six more priestesses each wearing a Mooncloth Robe.
rrf_elser.response:
# Illidan Stormrage

**Illidan is one of World of Warcraft's most iconic and complex characters, known for his relentless determination to save Azeroth at any cost.** He's the demon hunter who became the master of the Black Temple and led the Illidari against the Burning Legion. His defining trait is an unwillingness to compromise—he believed that saving the world required absolute dedication and sacrifice, even when others questioned his methods.

**His personal story is deeply tied to his twin brother Malfurion and his love for Tyrande.** While Malfurion dreamed of what Azeroth could become, Illidan chose to fight for what it actually was. Despite his feelings for Tyrande, he respected her choice to be with Malfurion and held her as his anchor through times of doubt. In his final moments, he acknowledged that all his paths had led to a single purpose: ensuring Azeroth's survival.

**Illidan is also remembered as the final boss of the Black Temple**, where adventurers confronted him wielding his legendary Warglaives of Azzinoth. His legacy continued even after his death, as his soul and body became central to storylines involving the Naaru, the recovery of his essence, and the prevention of Sargeras's return to Azeroth.

rrf_elser.question: What dropped from Onyxia?
rrf_elser.reference: Onyxia is a black dragon who is the daughter of Deathwing. She dropped tier 2 headpieces for all classes, the Onyxia Scale Cloak, and had a chance to drop the Deathbringer sword and the Vis'kag the Bloodletter. She was also known for dropping a very rare mount called Onyxia's Lair.
snippets: Onyxia: Onyxia serves as one of the two secondary antagonists (alongside her brother Nefarian) in classic World of Warcraft and one of the supporting antagonists in Cataclysm. Biography Chronormu's visage thumb|Onyxia in Hearthstone. Onyxia believed that humans were the mortal race that posed the greatest threat to dragonkind. When deciding on her mortal guise, she chose the form of an attractive female human so that she could manipulate their kind and allow her to seize all she wanted from them. At some point, the bronze dragon Chronormu—who could not make up his mind on what mortal form to choose during his upcoming Visage Day ceremony—sought an audience with Onyxia in the caverns of the Obsidian Dragonshrine, where the daughter of Deathwing was overseeing drakonid laborers and sparring drakes. Onyxia scoffed at Chronormu's view that dragons chose a visage to better relate to the mortal races, stating that it sounded "like something Nozdormu would teach his whelps". She assumed her human form and continued that no visage could change a dragon's nature as a hunter and killer. Like their other abilities, shapeshifting was a tool to take what they wanted from the young races. Though Chronormu protested that this was not how he wanted mortals to see him, Onyxia offered to let him stay by her side and teach him everything he needed to know about mortals and allow him to become the greatest member of his flight, great enough that even Nozdormu would kneel before him.

For strategy videos of both the 10-man and 25-man encounter, look below the loot table on this page. You ideally want 2 tanks, 2 healers, 3 melee DPS, and 3 ranged DPS for 10 man, and 2 tanks, 5 healers, 8 melee DPS, and 10 ranged DPS for 25 man. Onyxia's Lair: Onyxia's Lair is a level 80 raid dungeon located in the Wyrmbog, Dustwallow Marsh. It is home to Onyxia, the brood mother of the black dragonflight. Onyxia's Lair was originally a level 60, 40-player raid dungeon, one of two that were available upon release of World of Warcraft (the other being Molten Core). It was re-tuned as a level 80 raid dungeon in patch 3.2.2 in honor of World of Warcraft's 5-year anniversary. Like all other Wrath of the Lich King raid dungeons, it has both 10-man and 25-man modes. Lore Onyxia built the lair. The entrance to the lair was carved to look like the maw of the broodmother herself, with its inner pathway being magma-lined and the ground cracked, glowing with fire from beneath. The arching stone ceiling groans and shifts, creating constant rockfalls. The walls spill lava where the heat can't be contained.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 148 Onyxia is the daughter of the mighty Black Aspect, Deathwing, and sister of the scheming Nefarian, Lord of Blackrock Spire. Whereas her father favors brute force, and her brother creates twisted abominations to carry out his will, Onyxia prefers subtle manipulation, and delights in meddling in the political affairs of mortals, using her charm and power to influence such delicate matters between the different races to her own ends. Some believe that Onyxia has even assumed an alias once used by her father - the title of the royal House Prestor.

Beyond her head, the raid also dropped standard loot that would have been appropriate for the difficulty level at the time.

rrf_elser.question: What is Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker?
rrf_elser.reference: Thunderfury Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is a legendary one-handed sword from vanilla WoW. It required players to collect two bindings that dropped from Garr and Baron Geddon in Molten Core, then complete a quest chain involving Highlord Demitrian. The weapon is known for its powerful proc that reduces enemy threat generation and chains lightning to nearby enemies. It became a famous meme in the WoW community.
snippets: Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker: thumb|Thunderfury's owner, Wray. Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is the legendary sword once wielded by Prince Thunderaan, Prince of Air. Equipping the sword awards the Feat of Strength, Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (achievement). It is currently the only legendary weapon that is available to all classes. History Ragnaros the Firelord, and his lieutenants Garr and Baron Geddon, defeated Thunderaan, Prince of Air, after the First Age of Creation, during the Elemental Sundering. Ragnaros was unable to fully consume Thunderaan’s essence, so he forced what little remained into a talisman of elemental binding and shattered it. He gave the two halves to his lieutenants. Ages passed, and somehow the talisman halves found their way into the hands of Highlord Demitrian, a follower of Thunderaan. He recombined the talisman but was unable to release his master. Instead, he did the best he could: He crafted a blade to serve as a vessel for his master's essence. Nik the Mountain can be seen wielding the blade. When the Earthen Ring decides to resurrect Thunderaan to replace the fallen Al'Akir, a shaman adventurer takes Thunderfury from the corpse of its last wielder — a dwarf warrior called Wray who died while facing RagemawBlessed Blade of the Windseeker — in Azsuna and brings it to the Vortex Pinnacle.

Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker (quest item): Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker is formed through combining Damaged Thunderfury with the Heart of Skywall. Its power was used to bring Thunderaan back during the third invasion of the Burning Legion.World of Warcraft: Exploring Azeroth: Kalimdor, pg. 107 Objective of Recharging the Blade Patch changes References External links

rrf_elser.response:
# Thunderfury, Blessed Blade of the Windseeker

**Thunderfury is a legendary sword that contains the essence of Prince Thunderaan, the Prince of Air.** It's one of World of Warcraft's most iconic weapons, and notably, it's the only legendary weapon available to all classes, meaning anyone can equip it regardless of their specialization.

The blade has a complex history. After Ragnaros defeated Thunderaan during the Elemental Sundering, he couldn't fully consume the Prince's essence, so he trapped what remained in a talisman and shattered it into two pieces. Ages later, a follower named Highlord Demitrian recombined the talisman pieces and crafted Thunderfury as a vessel to contain his master's soul. Over time, the sword passed through various hands until a dwarf warrior named Wray carried it into the Broken Isles.

When the Earthen Ring decided to resurrect Thunderaan to replace the fallen Al'Akir, player shamans retrieved the blade from Wray's corpse in Azsuna and brought it to the Vortex Pinnacle, where it was combined with the Heart of Skywall to fully restore its power. The sword then served as the key to bringing the Windseeker back during the Legion invasion.
